systemStatus.headline: Mon Jul 20, 7 AM run. Toast and Sling BOTH LANDED -- Sunday Jul 19 net $12,115.62 (254 orders / 439 guests) is real, not a carry-forward. Two things need eyes: today's Sling schedule shows only 3 employees / 5.50 hours with 2 of 3 shifts malformed, which reads as UNPUBLISHED rather than light; and Google Ads spend is up while impressions, clicks and conversions are all down over 7 days.
systemStatus.detail:
Mon Jul 20 07:00 AM PT run. All four data sources refreshed; TripAdvisor has no data.

TOAST -- Sun Jul 19 DELIVERED. Net $12,115.62 / gross $12,708.30 on 254 orders and 439 guests, $27.60 per guest. Versus SDLW (Sun Jul 12, net $6,860.86) that is +76.6%, but Jul 12 was an unusually thin Sunday, so read the gain as a weak comparison base rather than a doubling of trade. Watch items: labor 19.9% of net, a fourth consecutive day above the 18-19% band; 29 void items, more than double Saturday's 12; 3 orders left open at close (up from 1); Julianna Ornelas and Harmony Willson both auto-clocked out.

SLING -- Mon Jul 20 delivered 7:01 AM, and the number is the problem. 3 employees / 3 shifts / 5.50 hours against Sunday's 15 employees / 74.82 hours. Rafael Patricio (5:06A-5:06A) and Casey Smith (5:34A-5:34A) are zero-length malformed shifts -- the same defect logged for Isael Salvador Narciso previously -- leaving Julianna Ornelas 6:30A-12:30P as the only genuine shift. Confirm the Monday schedule was published before acting on 5.50 hours. 0 uncovered, 0 OT.

GOOGLE ADS -- live scrape. Yesterday $58.63 / 7,047 impr / 178 clicks / 0 conversions. The 7-day picture is the concern: cost UP $20.37 while impressions are DOWN 10,646, clicks DOWN 96 and conversions DOWN 176.15. Only 2 of 4 campaigns run. 'World Cup HQ 2026' ($20/day) is now paused with the Final behind us -- archive it. 'Oldest SB Brewery on Tap' is paused with all asset groups limited by policy, and that same policy limit also sits on the live PMax-2 campaign next to 'Limited by budget'; the policy flag is the one worth clearing. No disapprovals.

YELP ADS -- live scrape. 30.2k ad impressions, 397 ad clicks, 137 ad leads (59% of 231) over 30 days. Jul 1-19 spend $264.51 at $0.99 CPC and $2.88 CPL. Bonus budget remaining is still not exposed anywhere on the dashboard, 6th run running -- unavailable, not zero.

TRIPADVISOR -- no analytics email in 24h or 7d; only consumer marketing blasts. No figures.

NOTE: this run refreshed sales, staffing and ad data only. Review, inbox and business-profile sections were not re-checked and carry their prior timestamps; nothing was posted or sent.

toast.source: GROUP-level Daily Performance Summary (The Brewhouse Santa Barbara Group), email delivered 6:30 AM PT Mon Jul 20.
toast.note: DELIVERED at the Mon Jul 20 7 AM run -- Sunday Jul 19 actuals, no longer a carry-forward. Net $12,115.62 on 254 orders / 439 guests. Up sharply vs SDLW (Sun Jul 12 net $6,860.86, +76.6%) -- but Jul 12 was an unusually thin Sunday (159 orders, only 2 takeout orders logged), so treat the percentage as flattered by a weak base rather than a true doubling. WATCH: labor 19.9% of net, still above the 18-19% band; 29 void items (up from 12 Saturday); 3 orders left open at close; Julianna Ornelas and Harmony Willson both auto-clocked out.

sling.note: TODAY (Mon Jul 20), email delivered 7:01 AM PT. 3 employees / 3 shifts / 5.50 hours, 0 uncovered, 0 OT. FLAG: this is a steep drop from Sunday's 15 employees / 74.82 hours and looks UNDER-PUBLISHED rather than a genuinely light Monday -- 2 of the 3 shifts (Rafael Patricio 5:06A-5:06A, Casey Smith 5:34A-5:34A) are zero-length malformed entries, the same defect seen previously, leaving Julianna Ornelas 6:30A-12:30P as the only real shift on the books. Verify the Monday schedule was actually published in Sling before trusting the 5.50-hour figure.

FLAG: this is a steep drop from Sunday's 15 employees / 74.82 hours and looks UNDER-PUBLISHED rather than a genuinely light Monday -- 2 of the 3 shifts (Rafael Patricio 5:06A-5:06A, Casey Smith 5:34A-5:34A) are zero-length malformed entries, the same defect seen previously, leaving Julianna Ornelas 6:30A-12:30P as the only real shift on the books. Verify the Monday schedule was actually published in Sling before trusting the 5.50-hour figure.

FLAG: this is a steep drop from Sunday's 15 employees / 74.82 hours and looks UNDER-PUBLISHED rather than a genuinely light Monday -- 2 of the 3 shifts (Rafael Patricio 5:06A-5:06A, Casey Smith 5:34A-5:34A) are zero-length malformed entries, the same defect seen previously, leaving Julianna Ornelas 6:30A-12:30P as the only real shift on the books. Verify the Monday schedule was actually published in Sling before trusting the 5.50-hour figure.

Spend is creeping up while output falls: yesterday $58.63 for 178 clicks and 0 recorded conversions, and the 7-day window shows cost UP $20.37 while impressions are DOWN 10,646, clicks DOWN 96 and conversions DOWN 176.15. Only 2 of 4 campaigns are live. 'World Cup HQ 2026' ($20/day) is now PAUSED and the Final has passed -- archive it and reclaim the budget line. 'Oldest SB Brewery on Tap' is paused with ALL ASSET GROUPS LIMITED BY POLICY, and the same policy limit is also flagged on the active PMax-2 campaign alongside 'Limited by budget' -- the policy restriction is the more actionable of the two. No disapprovals, no budget alerts.

Account daily budget total $58.37/day; 2 drafts still unpublished.

yelpAds.note: LIVE scrape Mon Jul 20 ~7:18 AM PT. Last 30d: 30.2k ad impressions (73% of 41.6k), 397 ad clicks, 518 page visits from ads (55% of 934), 137 leads from ads (59% of 231). Billing Jul 1-19: $264.51, CPC $0.99, CPL $2.88 -- $13.92/day, drifting up slightly. Day over day Yelp added $22.94 of spend, 0 additional ad clicks and 3 leads. COST COMPARISON: Yelp CPC $0.99 is 3x Google's $0.33, though Yelp's $2.88 cost-per-lead is the cleaner number to judge it on since Google is recording no conversions at all. Bonus budget remaining is STILL not surfaced anywhere on the dashboard (6th consecutive run) -- treat as unavailable, not zero.

reviews.lastUpdated: 2026-07-20 -- Mon Jul 20 4 AM run. Gmail delta on businessprofile-noreply@google.com + no-reply@spothopperapp.com. BOTH listings checked.
reviews.note: BOTH GOOGLE LISTINGS CHECKED. Direct load of the Unreplied tabs again returned Google's search-embedded merchant view, so review lists were sourced from Gmail notifications. THE BREWHOUSE: 2 unreplied -- Ronald Johnson (3 stars, Jul 19, World Cup Final: no sightlines + flat beer) and Thuy Anh Le (5 stars, photo-only, Jul 17). BREWHOUSE COFFEE: 1 unreplied -- Christie Hahn (5 stars, Jul 19, 'A Perfect 10/10 Experience'). Coffee had ZERO unreplied yesterday, so this is genuinely new. Joe (5 stars, Jul 17) and Matthew (Jul 16) were already answered. Draft replies for all 3 are in the Mon Jul 20 brief. NOTHING POSTED -- Joal approves every reply.

unanswered.author: Ronald Johnson
unanswered.snippet: Went for the world cup final got there a little late but the place had tables with no direct view of the game. Secondly just ordered burgers and a pitcher of beer, burgers were OK, beer was flat. Thankfully I was in with good company and none of that mattered
